item.title: El milagro de Paraguay: de país ignorado a imán de inversiones
item.description: Empresarios latinoamericanos llegan en masa, elegantes torres se elevan sobre la antes adormecida capital y su presidente conservador estrecha vínculos con EE.UU.

item.title: An Underdog Trump Ally in Latin America Becomes an Investor Magnet
item.description: Entrepreneurs flock to Paraguay from across the region as sleek towers rise above the once-sleepy capital and its conservative president cozies up to the US.

item.title: Fosun Secures $500 Million Refinancing Loan Despite Loss Warning
item.description: Chinese conglomerate Fosun International Ltd. has secured a refinancing loan package of around $500 million despite recently warning that its preliminary annual loss could widen by as much as fivefold.

item.title: Bangladesh Seeks $2 Billion Loans by June to Fund Fuel Imports
item.description: Bangladesh is seeking about $2 billion in loans from multilateral lenders by June as the new government looks to finance imports of liquefied natural gas and other fuels during the summer, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: J D Wetherspoon Slumps After Second Profit Warning This Year
item.description: J D Wetherspoon Plc shares dropped after the pub operator issued its second profit warning this year, citing weaker consumer finances and rising costs.

item.title: ByteDance Agrees to Sell Moonton to Savvy Games for $6 Billion
item.description: ByteDance Ltd. has agreed to sell games unit Moonton to Savvy Games Group for $6 billion, unloading a gaming studio it acquired just a few years ago to focus on generative AI.

item.title: ECB Totally Determined to Meet Inflation Target, Villeroy Says
item.description: The European Central Bank is determined to meet its inflation goal, Governing Council member Francois Villeroy de Galhau said.

item.title: Patriot Supplier Vincorion’s Shares Rise After German IPO
item.description: Vincorion SE’s shares rallied in their Frankfurt trading debut, as the German supplier of power systems for military vehicles joined Europe’s growing ranks of defense stocks.

item.title: Asian LNG Buyers Monitor Tense Market on Lengthy Qatar Outage
item.description: Asian buyers were closely monitoring a fraught liquefied natural gas market after Qatar announced that two trains at the world’s largest export hub could stay offline for as long as five years, tightening global supplies.

item.title: US Yields Continue to Push Higher as Inflation Fears Persist
item.description: US Treasury yields continued their advance higher Friday as markets reacted to hawkish comments from central banks and Brent crude oil stayed above $100 a barrel.

item.title: Greg Brew on Surging Energy and the 'Strategic Trap' of the War in Iran
item.description: This Is Iran's Strategy to win.

item.title: Odd Lots: Greg Brew on the US’s Strategic Trap in Iran (Podcast)
item.description: Odd Lots: Greg Brew on the US’s Strategic Trap in Iran (Podcast)【原文：Odd Lots: Greg Brew on the US’s Strategic Trap in Iran (Podcast)】

item.title: Markets Are Underpricing This Commodity Shock
item.description: Markets are underpricing the supply shock stemming from the interruption of oil flows through the Strait of Hormuz. Rates are too low, equities too high and the dollar not strong enough relative to the move in commodities.

item.title: Economists Pivot to See ECB Hikes While Diverging on First Move
item.description: Economists are shifting to align with the market view that European Central Bank interest-rate hikes are inevitable within months.

item.title: European Gas Falls as US, Israel Seek to Ease Iran War Concerns
item.description: European natural gas fell after Israel and the US sought to ease concerns about further attacks on Persian Gulf energy facilities.

item.title: Asia Turns to Coal as Iran War Rapidly Shrinks Supplies of Gas
item.description: War in the Middle East is forcing Asia to turn to coal to plug the gaping hole emerging in its supply of liquefied natural gas.

item.title: VinFast Risks North Carolina Clawback of US EV Plant Incentives
item.description: Pressure is mounting on VinFast Auto Ltd. after two US officials reminded the Vietnamese EV maker of its obligations under a four-year-old deal to build a mammoth plant in North Carolina.

item.title: Unilever Confirms Talks on McCormick Offer for Food Business
item.description: Unilever Plc said it’s in discussions to sell its food business to McCormick & Co. in a major shift for the maker of Hellmann’s mayonnaise.

item.title: UK Posts Bigger-Than-Forecast £14.3 Billion Budget Deficit
item.description: Britain borrowed more than forecast in February, showing the risks facing the public finances even before the war in Iran upended the economic outlook.

item.title: Iran Continues Gulf Strikes After Israel Vows to Spare Energy Sites
item.description: Damage following an Iranian strike in Tel Aviv, on March 19. Photographer: Erik Marmor/Getty Images

item.title: Novartis to Buy Breast Cancer Drug for as Much as $3 Billion
item.description: Novartis AG agreed to buy an experimental breast cancer drug from Synnovation Therapeutics for as much as $3 billion to bolster its oncology pipeline.

item.title: FTSE 100 to Steady After Bruising Session in European Stocks
Oil Market’s Seaborne Buffer Runs Down Fast as Iran War Drags On
item.description: The amount of oil stored at sea — a vital buffer for markets — is running down fast, as supply from the Persian Gulf remains constrained for a third week and buyers are forced to find quick alternatives.

item.title: ECB Would Need April Hike If Price Outlook Sours, Nagel Says
item.description: The European Central Bank will need to consider hiking interest rates as soon as next month if price pressures build further due to the Iran war, according to Governing Council member Joachim Nagel.

item.title: South Africa Imposes Anti-Dumping Duties on Chinese, Thai Steel
item.description: South Africa approved new import taxes to protect its steel industry after a probe found that cheap imports of some products from China and Thailand unfairly undercut local manufacturers.

item.title: Bollore’s Shock Dividend Has Investors Baffled About Endgame
item.description: Vincent Bolloré’s surprise proposal to pay out €4.2 billion ($4.8 billion) from the eponymous company sitting at the heart of his tangled empire has spawned a slew of theories about the French billionaire’s endgame.

item.title: US Soy Ships Pour Into China as Trump Summit Delay Clouds Trade
item.description: A wave of US soybeans reached Chinese shores this year as a deal between the two nations revived the trade. But the delay of President Donald Trump’s visit to Beijing is clouding prospects over how long the influx will last.

item.title: India’s Condolences for Ayatollah Cleared Way for Deal on Hormuz
item.description: Five days after the US and Israel killed Iran’s supreme leader, the top bureaucrat in India’s Foreign Ministry made his way to the Islamic Republic’s embassy on a tree-lined street in New Delhi.

item.title: China Pulls Silver From Global Markets to Meet Surging Demand
item.description: China’s ravenous appetite for silver lifted overseas purchases to an eight-year high at the start of 2026, as importers fed a surge in industrial and investment demand.

item.title: Malaysia Said to Tap Banks for First Dollar Bond Sale Since 2021
item.description: Malaysia has enlisted a group of banks to arrange a planned $1 billion dollar-bond sale for refinancing, marking its first return to the US currency market in five years.
